hooks.command: printf '%s\n' '[PM plugin] In this repo, Linear issue tracking and git branching are owned by a separate PM agent — your coding session does not self-manage either. Operating contract: STARTING WORK — before writing or committing ANY feature work you must be on the ticket branch the PM set up. Invoke /pm:build to pull the ticket, create its branch (fx-<n>-<slug>, one branch per ticket), and claim it In Progress. This fires the moment you are pointed at new work — INCLUDING a mid-session redirect ("now do X", "do the rest", "also handle Y"): treat that as a new unit of work and call /pm:build FIRST. If the new work is really several steps of one thing, it is one ticket; if it is genuinely separate tickets, start and finish them one at a time, each on its own branch. NEVER commit feature work to main or off the ticket branch; if you notice you have, stop and call /pm:build. DURING WORK — /pm:capture <idea> when a non-trivial out-of-scope idea or bug surfaces (file it, do not silently fix it). /pm:checkpoint <issue> at a commit boundary to have the PM commit staged work and log progress. /pm:plan when you must diverge from the ticket, hit cross-ticket impact, or face a decision that needs user buy-in — route it through the PM instead of deciding alone. FINISHING — /pm:done <issue> when the work is complete or its acceptance is approved; do NOT self-merge or close issues, the PM reconciles and closes. Keep the session scoped to the claimed ticket; route everything else through the PM.'
